title: ballota rugosa
intro: Ballota rugosa, also known as the narrow-leaved horehound, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its small, pinkish-purple flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as digestive problems like indigestion and nausea. The health benefits of Ballota rugosa include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help reduce inflammation and combat infections. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to soothe respiratory and digestive issues.
